organization.name: Samsung Electronics
organization.short_description:
Samsung Electronics is a global leader in technology and consumer electronics, known for its wide range of products that include semiconductors, home appliances, telecommunications devices, and display technologies. Established in 1969 as a subsidiary of the Samsung Group, the company has evolved from manufacturing black-and-white televisions to becoming a prominent multinational technology conglomerate.

The product portfolio of Samsung Electronics encompasses consumer electronics such as televisions, washing machines, refrigerators, air conditioners, and audio equipment. The company has made significant advancements in semiconductors, becoming the world's largest producer of memory chips. Samsung is also a major player in the mobile phone market, having introduced its first mobile phone in 1992 and producing over 800 million devices to date. Additionally, Samsung is recognized for its innovative display technology and offers information technology services through Samsung SDS. The company invests heavily in research and development to drive innovation across various industries.
